body:
When trying to output diagnostics for 1D vertical column fields in Atmos (e.g., vertical profiles of scalar statistics) using a `NetCDFWriter` that was initialized from a 3D `ExtrudedFiniteDifferenceSpace`, the writer would fail with:

`ERROR: Incompatible z dimension already exists in file output/.../variable_name.nc`

In `write_field!`, when calling `add_space_coordinates_maybe!`, the full `writer.num_points` tuple (e.g., (num_lon, num_lat, num_z)) was passed for all space types. However, for `FiniteDifferenceSpace` (1D column), only the vertical dimension (num_z,) should be passed.

The `add_space_coordinates_maybe!` method for `FiniteDifferenceSpace` then called `dimension_exists(nc, name, num_points_z)` with the full 3-tuple, causing the dimension size check to fail.


This PR adds conditional logic in `write_field!` to extract only the vertical component of `num_points` when the field's space is a `FiniteDifferenceSpace`. This mirrors the existing pattern for handling 2D horizontal fields, where `num_points` is appropriately sliced.
title: Support 1D column (FiniteDifferenceSpace) diagnostics when writer is initialized from 3D space
